MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Nathan L. Winegar, Esq., Winegar Legal P.C., (650) 226-3087, nathan@winegarlegal.com, for Defendant and Cross-Defendant WNC Insurance Services, Inc.
Keith Davis, Esq., THE RODARTI GROUP, PC, (949) 831-0303, kdavis@constructionriskmanagers.com for Defendant Bryant Street Holding,

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

CROSS-DEFENDANT WNC INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S (CONSOLIDATED CASE #) DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendant WNC Insurance Services, Inc. ("WNC") demurrer to cross-complainant Bryant Street Holdings' ("Bryant Street") first amended cross-complaint is sustained with leave to amend.

WNC's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to Bryant Street's third cause of action for "breach of contractual duty to properly investigate a claim" and fourth cause of action for "breach of contractual duty to pay a covered insurance claim." Bryant Street is granted leave to allege the existence of a valid contract between the parties. The cross-complaint incorporates 352 pages of insurance policies (see Exhibit 8) but does not state which policies or provisions apply to Bryant Street and WNC. Accordingly, it is not clear from the face of the cross-complaint whether "properly investigating a claim" is a duty WNC owes to Bryant Street or whether WNC had a "duty to pay a covered insurance claim." The Court does not consider Bryant Street's new argument that Mr. Flitton is WNC's agent, but Bryant Street is granted leave to amend to make such allegations.

WNC's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to Bryant Street's fifth cause of action for "breach of the duty of good faith and fair dealing" sixth cause of action for declaratory judgment for the same reasons stated above. The existence of a valid contract is an essential element of Bryant Street's fifth cause of action, and it is unclear from the face of the cross-complaint whether an actual controversy exists.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Amanda Manahan, Esq., The Rodarti Group, PC. 949-831-0303, for Defendant Bryant Street Holdings LLC.
Colleen Beverly, Esq., Calusen Miller P.C., 312-855-1010, cbeverly@clausen.com; for Defendant Liberty Surplus Insurance Corporation.

Matter is not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT LIBERTY SURPLUS INSURACE CORPORATION'S CONSOLIDATED DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendant Liberty Surplus Insurance Co.'s ("Liberty") demurrer to cross-complainant Bryant Street Holdings' ("Bryant Street") first amended cross-complaint is overruled.

Liberty's demurrer is overruled as to Bryant Street's fourth cause of action for "breach of contractual duty to pay a covered insurance claim." The cross-complaint alleges the existence of a valid contract, breach of that contract by Liberty, and resulting damages to Bryant Street. The reservation of rights letter does not contradict Bryant Street's allegation that Liberty improperly withheld payment for construction delays allegedly caused by mold growth.

Liberty's demurrer is overruled as to Bryant Street's fifth cause of action for "breach of the duty of good faith and fair dealing." The cross-complaint alleges: the existence of a valid contract; conditions precedent to Liberty's performance under the contract (construction delays from mold growth); unfair interference with Bryant Street's rights to receive the benefits of the contract (improper investigation leading to failure to pay delay damages); and harm to Bryant Street.

Liberty's demurrer is overruled as to Bryant Street's sixth cause of action for declaratory judgment. The cross complaint alleges an actual controversy between the parties.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com mailto:maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
